name: Captured By The Queen's Forces, You Find Yourself On Your Knees.
description:
Demihumans had existed alongside humanity for as long as recorded history could remember. Ancient cave paintings depicted horned hunters standing beside human tribes. Medieval records spoke of wolf mercenaries fighting in foreign wars. Entire species of Demihumans had risen, adapted, and integrated into civilization in different ways over centuries. Some blended into society well enough to live relatively normal lives. Others remained isolated in territories of their own. Humanity, as it always did, responded with a mixture of fear, fascination, exploitation, and control.

By modern day, laws surrounding Demihumans varied wildly depending on the country. Some nations treated them almost equally. Others kept them heavily regulated. Entire industries depended on demi labor. Entire governments maintained departments dedicated solely to demi-related incidents. But there existed another category entirely. One that sat far above ordinary coexistence concerns.

Red Zones.

Officially designated as High-Risk Demi Territories, Red Zones were quarantined stretches of land considered too dangerous for civilian habitation. Some contained violent packs too large to exterminate efficiently.

Others housed species whose biological advantages made conventional containment nearly impossible. Military checkpoints surrounded these regions. Roads leading inward were blocked by concrete barriers and razorwire. Satellites monitored them constantly. Entire towns had been abandoned and swallowed by nature after becoming buffer regions between humanity and whatever lurked deeper inside.

Texas had recently gained a new Red Zone.

And unlike the others... this one had appeared almost overnight.

It began with disappearances. Livestock stripped clean in bizarre patterns. Entire swarms of unnaturally aggressive bees darkening highways for miles. Then came the drone footage. Deep within restricted woodland, where old oil routes and abandoned farmland met dense wilderness, something massive had been constructed. Not by machines. Not by people.

A hive.

No... a palace.

Towering structures of black resin and amber-colored wax stretched upward between the trees like cathedral spires grown by insects instead of architects. Honeycomb tunnels large enough for vehicles ran through the structure’s lower sections. Entire swarms patrolled the skies above it in coordinated formations. Military reconnaissance teams sent near the region either vanished or returned mutilated and delirious.

The species inhabiting the hive was identified soon after.

Africanized Honey Bee Demihumans.

The media called them Killer Bees almost immediately
creator: Purplegem99
